ddls:
CREATE TABLE "indicators" (
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each indicator */,
  "global_category" TEXT /* Global category of the indicator (e.g., Adaptation, Mitigation) */,
  "indicator_name" TEXT /* Name of the specific indicator (e.g., LULUCF/Forestry) */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the indicator (e.g., No specified measure, LULUCF/Forestry: General) */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Count of the indicator occurrences */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the indicator value */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the indicator */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the indicator */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the indicator data */,
  "update_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which the indicator data is updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sectors" (
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sector */,
  "sector_name" TEXT /* Name of the sector (e.g., LULUCF/Forestry, Sustainable Land Management) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sector */,
  "parent_sector_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent sector (for hierarchical sector relationships) */,
  "sector_code" TEXT /* Unique code for the sector */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "indicator_sectors" (
  "indicator_sector_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each indicator-sector relationship */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the indicator */,
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sector */,
  "value" INTEGER /* Value of the indicator in the sector */,
  "sector_weight" REAL /* Weightage of the sector in the indicator calculation */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the indicator-sector relationship */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the indicator-sector relationship */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_sector_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_indicator_sectors_sector_id FOREIGN KEY ("sector_id") REFERENCES sectors ("sector_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_indicator_sectors_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES indicators ("indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measures" (
  "measure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measure */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the indicator */,
  "measure_name" TEXT /* Name of the specific measure (e.g., LULUCF/Forestry: General) */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Count of the measure occurrences */,
  "measure_code" TEXT /* Unique code for the measure */,
  "measure_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the measure */,
  "measure_unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the measure */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measure_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measures_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES indicators ("indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region (e.g., Asia, Europe) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "region_code" TEXT /* Unique code for the region */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code for the region (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "indicator_regions" (
  "indicator_region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each indicator-region relationship */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the indicator */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region */,
  "value" INTEGER /* Value of the indicator in the region */,
  "region_weight" REAL /* Weightage of the region in the indicator calculation */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the indicator-region relationship */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the indicator-region relationship */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_indicator_regions_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES indicators ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_indicator_regions_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed for security) */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the user account was created */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the indicator being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the indicator */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the indicator was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_method" TEXT /* Method of access (e.g., API, web interface) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES indicators ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the report */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the report */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the report was created */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the report */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV) */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, published) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_indicators" (
  "report_indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report-indicator relationship */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the indicator */,
  "value" INTEGER /* Value of the indicator in the report */,
  "indicator_weight" REAL /* Weightage of the indicator in the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_indicators_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES indicators ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_indicators_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id")
)
